rows.source_ref: "Never Lose Again" thread by Erebus (with kleybenny, driven18, notabdullah1, T721), ForexFactory thread 1371892, 2025-2026, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1371892/never-lose-again
rows.concept: Break of the previous week's high/low in the direction of the prevailing weekly bias, held as a swing with SL at the recent swing point and all positions flat by Friday close.
rows.eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 prior-week high/low breakout with a stop and Friday-flat exit; FX/metals data on .DWX; discretionary overlay (bias, swing SL, ADR TP moves) must be mechanized.
rows.notes: Entry anchor (prior-week high/low breakout) and Friday-close exit are objective; SL 'at recent swing point', 'weekly bias', and 'move TP daily using ADR targets' are explicitly discretionary (author: 'not a system with hard entries', 'individual discretion'). Risk <=2%/position, initial RR 1:1 scalable to daily/weekly/monthly ADR targets, later variants 3:1 + trailing stop. Maps directly onto the already-explored Balke weekly-range-breakout family (a poster even calls it 'Good Ole Range Breakout... for a weekly as a range') which the shop found edge-real but Q05-DD-fatal and non-generalizing -> low novelty. Live evidence is a small ~3-week journal; author repeatedly flags Gold as the losing leg (FX-only did better). Attached 'Previous Week High Low.mq4' only plots the levels, not a strategy. Secondary forum chatter (Dow Futures 5m breakout, 'go opposite gold breakouts') are undefined asides, not separate candidates. Forum-source reputability caveat.
